channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Ukraine says more than 150 Chinese mercenaries are fighting for Russia in Ukraine
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine on Wednesday expanded on its claim that significant numbers of Chinese nationals are fighting for Russia’s invading army, saying it had gathered detailed intelligence on more than 150 mercenaries Moscow allegedly recruited through social media.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Rumor of monkey trade for child leads to Missouri foster mom's arrest
item.description: Officials say new child abuse charges filed against a Winfield woman who has fostered children for a decade are "just the tip of the iceberg.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mueller, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump administration will screen for antisemitic activity in granting immigration benefits
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump 's administration will immediately start considering “antisemitic activity” on social media and physical harassment of Jewish people as grounds for denying immigration benefits, according to a federal agency announcement on Wednesday. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump orders probe into 'Anonymous' New York Times editorial writer
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday signed a pair of memorandums directing the Department of Justice to investigate two individuals who served in his first administration and pushed back against Trump.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Brawl erupts in Kansas courtroom during murder sentencing
item.description: The murder victim's mother spoke to the judge before the sentencing. Her words sparked anger among some of the other people in the courtroom.
item.dc:creator: Carina Branson, NewsNation

item.title: Woman can testify Weinstein used force to rape her despite his acquittal, judge rules
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A woman who says Harvey Weinstein trapped her in a Manhattan hotel room and raped her in 2013 can use the word “force” when she testifies at his retrial even though the disgraced movie mogul was acquitted of using physical force against her, a judge ruled Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 1,700 pounds of butter recalled over bacteria contamination
item.description: Nearly a ton of salted butter is being recalled because, according to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration, the product is "contaminated with elevated levels of coliform," a bacteria found in animals' digestive tracts and waste.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pennsylvania judge is convicted of shooting and wounding her ex-boyfriend as he slept
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A magistrate judge in Pennsylvania accused of shooting and wounding her ex-boyfriend in the head as he slept was convicted Wednesday of attempted homicide and aggravated assault charges.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sam's Club plans to open 15 new locations a year, CEO says
item.description: Sam's Club CEO Chris Nicholas says the company plans to open 15 new locations a year "for the foreseeable future.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A California soccer coach charged in a 13-year-old's death is accused of assaulting other teens
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A Southern California youth soccer coach charged with killing a 13-year-old boy last month has also been accused of assaulting at least two other teens in recent years, prosecutors said.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Johnson aiming for budget vote Wednesday, but suggests support is not yet there
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) said Wednesday that he’s still aiming to stage a vote later in the evening on a Senate-passed budget bill to guide President Trump’s domestic agenda, but suggested the support is not yet there to pass it and the final vote will come later than 5:30 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump replaces Patel with Army secretary as ATF director
item.description: FBI Director Kash Patel has been removed as the acting director of the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump tariffs worry US shoppers who buy groceries at Asian supermarkets
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Loyal customers of Asian supermarkets and other grocery stores that specialize in selling imported food heaved a collective sigh of dismay when President Donald Trump announced extra-high U.S. tariffs on goods from dozens of countries.
item.dc:creator: TERRY TANG and KARENA PHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judges temporarily block 5 Venezuelans' deportations under Alien Enemies Act
item.description: Federal judges in New York and Texas temporarily blocked the Trump administration from deporting five Venezuelan men it alleges are gang members by using the Alien Enemies Act.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: ICE allowed back onto NYC's Rikers Island prison, with restrictions
item.description: The Eric Adams administration is permitting ICE to return to Rikers Island to help carry out criminal investigations into organized violence and migrant gangs like MS-13 and Tren de Aragua.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Americans convicted in Congo of a botched coup attempt now face US charges
item.description: Three Americans repatriated to the United States from Congo have been charged by the U.S. Justice Department with staging an elaborate coup attempt aimed at overthrowing the African nation’s government.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Schoenbaum And Eric Tucker, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump administration appeals court decision ordering AP journalists back into White House events
item.description: The Trump administration says it will appeal a federal court decision that ordered it to readmit Associated Press journalists to White House events on First Amendment grounds.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: NYC lets immigration officials open an office at Rikers jail, a priority for Trump
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams ' administration is allowing federal immigration officials to operate at Rikers Island to work on gang and drug-related criminal investigations in the city's largest lockup.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Microsoft says it's 'slowing or pausing' some AI data center projects, including $1B plan for Ohio
item.description: Microsoft says it's “slowing or pausing” some data center construction, including a $1 billion project in Ohio.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump limits tariffs on most nations for 90 days, except China
item.description: Facing a global market meltdown, President Donald Trump on Wednesday abruptly backed down on his tariffs on most nations for 90 days, but raised his tax rate on Chinese imports to 125%.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump limits tariffs on most nations for 90 days, except China
item.description: Facing a global market meltdown, President Donald Trump on Wednesday abruptly backed down on his tariffs on most nations for 90 days, but raised his tax rate on Chinese imports to 125%.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Florida kayak tour guide allegedly killed woman and ‘hid’ body inside room at Key Largo resort: officials
item.description: A man in Florida was arrested on Monday after he allegedly killed a woman and hid her body inside his hotel room at a Key Largo resort, according to officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Are dire wolves de-extinct? Genetically engineered pups spark controversy
item.description: Three genetically engineered wolves that may resemble extinct dire wolves are in an undisclosed secure location in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Didn't get a Trader Joe's mini pastel tote? You can buy one for over $1,000 on eBay
item.description: And as they did last year, customers were quick to snag the bags. If you weren't among them, you're in luck — but only if you're willing to spend a pretty penny.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, KRON4

item.title: A pipeline company filed hundreds of lawsuits against landowners. Now its project is threatened
item.description: MANSFIELD, S.D. (AP) — Jared Bossly was planting soybeans one spring night in 2023 on his 2,000-acre farm in South Dakota when he spotted a sheriff’s vehicle parked at the corner of his property. He had a hunch it wasn’t a social visit.
item.dc:creator: BY ERIC FERKENHOFF/LEE ENTERPRISES and JOSH KELETY/AP, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Teen seen shoving guns through border wall into Mexico
item.description: An Arizona teen allegedly was caught slipping guns and knives through the border wall south into Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Fernie Ortiz, Fox 5

item.title: Wild fish tale: Kayaker takes on 140-pound tuna in the Outer Banks
item.description: A man in the Outer Banks is riding a high after a true tuna tale that, for him, is the fishing catch of a lifetime.
item.dc:creator: Madison Pearman, NewsNation

item.title: Kennedy’s moves on measles don't quell criticism
item.description: The outbreak represents the first time Kennedy has had to reconcile his past as a leading critic of vaccines and his leadership of the federal health establishment.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KRON4

item.title: More than 900 illegal aliens charged with immigration-related crimes during first week of April: DOJ
item.description: US attorneys in six districts filed immigration-related charges against nearly 1,000 illegal aliens during the first week of April under Operation Take Back America, the DOJ said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Mexico governor mobilizes National Guard to tackle crime emergency in Albuquerque
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — Gov. Michelle Lujan Grisham on Tuesday declared a state of emergency in New Mexico’s largest city, saying that a significant increase in crime in Albuquerque warrants the help of the New Mexico National Guard.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Hearing looks at rules for evidence and other details in Bryan Kohberger's quadruple murder trial
item.description: Prosecutors and attorneys for a man charged in the killings of four University of Idaho students in 2022 began arguing some of the final ground rules they want for Bryan Kohberger’s trial.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Boone, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Human remains found in 1973 identified as missing Kansas teen
item.description: Human remains found near Garnett, Kansas, in 1973 have been positively identified more than 50 years later.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dulle, NewsNation

item.title: Surgery nightmare: Hospital admits mistake after failed cancer surgery attempt
item.description: An investigation is underway at MountainView Hospital in Las Vegas after a cancer patient woke up to startling news in the operating room.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Murphy, WGN 9

item.title: Outrage builds as video shows Idaho police shooting a knife-wielding autistic teenager
item.description: Idaho police officers opened fire from behind a chain-link fence just seconds after exiting their patrol cars and critically wounded a teenage boy — described by his family as nonverbal, autistic and intellectually disabled — as he stepped toward them with a knife, video from a witness shows.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, CLAUDIA LAUER and MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
